revision_reason: User feedback: previous role list was too narrow; added discovery/intent phase; expanded role taxonomy
objective: Build an open-source Python package and CLI tool named 'coder-waifu' that orchestrates an autonomous multi-agent engineering swarm inside Hermes. Before implementing any project task, coder-waifu enters a structured discovery phase in which it asks targeted clarifying questions and performs lightweight research to understand the user's actual intent. Only after that phase does it spawn role-based agent pairs to execute the work adversarially until production-grade standards are met. The system is context-aware, autonomously checkpoints before context compaction, and is resumable across sessions. Installable via pip for anyone using Hermes. The Coder-Waifu cute persona appears only in user-facing CLI output; all source code, artifacts, and documentation remain professional.
deliverables: CLI entry point: 'coder-waifu' command (e.g., 'coder-waifu run <task>', 'coder-waifu init')
Agent orchestration engine leveraging Hermes delegate_task API for spawning role-based agent pairs
Built-in role definitions covering a full professional engineering organization; each role spawns 2 collaborating agents for adversarial review
Mandatory discovery/intent-extraction phase: clarifying questions + lightweight research before any implementation spawn
Autonomous context checkpointing and resumption mechanism
pytest test suite with passing tests covering orchestration logic, role loading, checkpoint behavior, and CLI entry points
README.md with install instructions, usage examples, role overview, contribution guide, and architecture summary
.agent/ workflow artifacts: plan.json, state.json, evidence.json, review.json, architecture.md, decisions.md, context-summary.md, last-verification.json, activity-log.jsonl
deliverables_user_facing: A CLI that asks clarifying questions naturally before doing irreversible or scoped work
A CLI that researches the user's project domain before assuming technology choices
A CLI that produces professional engineering artifacts, not trial-and-error prototypes
constraints: Primary runtime dependency is Hermes Agent (delegate_task, subagent spawning). No custom orchestration runtime from scratch.
Minimal third-party runtime dependencies; prefer stdlib + Hermes built-ins. Only add deps with clear justification.
Discovery phase must execute before any implementation spawn: at least one round of clarifying questions or documented research should precede agent dispatch.
Context checkpointing must be autonomous and trigger before compaction, at task boundaries, and on explicit /compact signal.
Checkpoint state must survive session restart/resume.
All public APIs must have type hints.
License must be OSI-approved open source.
Code, commit messages, architecture documents, tests, and logs must be professional and persona-free.
Coder-Waifu cute persona appears only in direct user-facing CLI output, never in source code or artifacts.
Discovery questions MUST present selectable options first, with an explicit freeform Other option. Freeform must always remain available even when selectable options are presented.

discovery_phase.description: Before spawning any role-based agents, coder-waifu must run a structured discovery phase to understand intent, assumptions, and constraints.
steps: 1. Parse the user's request and identify explicit deliverables, constraints, and non-goals.
2. Identify ambiguities that materially affect scope, technology, timeline, security, or acceptance criteria. Ask targeted clarifying questions (one at a time or in a small coherent group).

3. Perform lightweight domain research: technology/language/framework official docs, known best practices, common pitfalls in the problem domain. Prefer primary sources (official docs) within the last 6 months. Mark unverified claims as inference.
4. Synthesize research into a draft contract with explicit reversible assumptions where information is unavailable or provisional.
5. Present the draft contract to the user and wait for explicit approval before proceeding.
6. Record approved contract, assumptions, and evidence to .agent/.
discovery_phase.blocking_questions_criteria: Questions are blocking only when different answers materially change scope, architecture, security, cost, irreversible actions, or acceptance criteria.
discovery_phase.reversible_assumptions_rule: Non-blocking uncertainties must be recorded as explicit reversible assumptions, not asked as questions.

non_goals: No standalone orchestration runtime for non-Hermes environments
No web UI, dashboard, or GUI
No database or persistent backend service
No support for non-Hermes agent frameworks
No automatic PyPI publishing (manual release only)
No mandatory role is required for every run; coder-waifu selects applicable roles per task
user_approvals_needed: Package layout (flat module vs. package) — assumption A1
Open source license choice (MIT, Apache 2.0, etc.) — assumption A2
Whether to include a default project scaffold template (coder-waifu init) — assumption A3
acceptance_criteria: pip install coder-waifu succeeds in a clean venv
coder-waifu --help displays available commands, roles, and discovery-phase behavior
Running 'coder-waifu run <task>' enters a discovery phase with clarifying questions or documented research before spawning agents
Discovery phase produces a draft contract presented to the user for approval before implementation begins
Approved contract is persisted to .agent/contract.json and gates all subsequent agent spawns
Each selected role spawns exactly 2 collab/review agents via Hermes delegate_task
Context checkpointing triggers automatically before compaction and writes to .coder-waifu/checkpoints/
A paused/resumed session continues from the last checkpoint without losing task state
README.md contains install instructions, CLI usage, role descriptions, discovery-phase overview, and a usage example
All source code and artifacts are professional and persona-free; Coder-Waifu persona is confined to user-facing CLI output
verification: pip install -e . succeeds in clean venv
coder-waifu run <trivial-task> produces a draft contract to .agent/contract.json and presents it to the user before spawning (manual observation)
Git status shows only intended files

A1_package_layout.description: Python package layout with package-level __init__.py. Supports future submodules: roles/, cli/, orchestration/, checkpointing/.

A2_license.description: OSI-approved MIT License. Reversible: can be changed to Apache 2.0 or another OSI-approved license before first release if requested.

A3_scaffold_template.description: Provides 'coder-waifu init <project-name>' that scaffolds a starter project with .coder-waifu/, README, and optional config. Reversible: can be removed before v1 if scope grows too large.

A4_hermes_api.description: Uses Hermes delegate_task API for all agent spawning. Assumes Hermes runtime provides this API in Python context.

A5_checkpoint_location.description: Checkpoints stored as JSON files in the target project's .coder-waifu/ directory. Avoids conflicts with .agent/ used by the orchestrator itself.

A6_role_definition_source.chosen: Bundled YAML files under coder_waifu/roles/ with user-extensible override support
A6_role_definition_source.description: Each built-in role defined as a YAML file specifying system prompt, expertise areas, review criteria, and agent count (always 2). Users can add custom YAML roles in their .coder-waifu/roles/ to override or extend the built-ins.

A7_context_trigger.chosen: Hermes context-compaction signal + task boundary + explicit /compact command
A7_context_trigger.description: Checkpoint triggers: (1) before context compaction, (2) at end of each agent task boundary, (3) on user-issued /compact. Assumes Hermes provides a compaction-trigger hook or signal. Fallback: keyword-based heuristics in coder-waifu if no direct hook is available.

A8_discovery_depth.chosen: At least one round of clarifying questions or documented lightweight research before any implementation spawn
A8_discovery_depth.description: The discovery phase must produce a draft contract with explicit assumptions. The CLI will present this draft and wait for user approval (or auto-approve when no blocking questions remain and the user has previously authorized 'coder-waifu continue without asking').
